message:
fix(cancel/symbol_cancel): clamp cancelled_at to avoid u64::MAX sentinel collision

cancelled_at uses u64::MAX as a 'not yet recorded' sentinel.  A
legitimate now.as_nanos() == u64::MAX would store the sentinel itself,
causing cancelled_at() to return None (or silently spin in the slow
path) for the cancelled token.

Clamp the stored value to u64::MAX - 1 at both store sites so the
sentinel cannot collide with a real timestamp.  Documented the
sentinel semantics on the field.

Also records audit batch qnsi2k.3 results: 14 files reviewed across
codec, util, time, sync, runtime, channel, types — 13 SOUND, 1 fix
(this file). The wheel.rs fix in the same batch was already merged
in 9d716105.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
